requirements.text: The system shall provide visible entry points into the careers experience from both general Six Flags pages and park-specific pages.
requirements.annotation_notes: The sequence shows a general site, a jobs-focused page, and a White Water park page with hiring content, indicating multiple user-facing paths into job discovery.
requirements.manual_verification_notes: the entry point in the main web page is on the top menu under "Jobs" and not directly in the main page integrated

requirements.text: The system shall support choosing a specific park to scope job discovery to a particular location.
requirements.annotation_notes: Multiple screens show park selection as a central step, including a generic park chooser and a jobs page that becomes scoped to Six Flags White Water.

requirements.text: The system shall carry the selected park context into subsequent recruiting screens and park-specific job content.
requirements.annotation_notes: After park selection, later screens reference Six Flags White Water and then show a White Water Atlanta lifeguard posting, indicating location context persists across the flow.

requirements.text: The system shall provide an individual detail page for a specific job opening.
requirements.annotation_notes: The final screen is a dedicated role page for a Lifeguard position, showing that openings can be represented as standalone job-detail views.

requirements.text: The system shall let applicants browse opportunities by department or specialized team within a selected park.
requirements.annotation_notes: The White Water page exposes team-oriented job areas such as lifeguards, food and beverage, park services, admissions, security, retail, and more opportunities.

requirements.text: The system shall highlight featured or currently hiring roles on park recruiting pages to speed job discovery.
requirements.annotation_notes: The park page prominently promotes hiring content such as NOW HIRING LIFEGUARDS, category cards, and a mega hiring event, which suggests featured discovery support.

requirements.text: The system shall present core metadata for each opening, including role title, job category, requisition identifier, employment type, location, and posting information.
requirements.annotation_notes: The job detail page visibly includes structured opening information beyond the title, which supports a requirement around transparent job metadata.

requirements.text: The system shall present role responsibilities, qualification criteria, and compensation information to support application decisions.
The system shall provide a prominent action for starting an application from recruiting content and job-opening pages.
requirements.annotation_notes: Apply Now calls to action are visible on the park recruiting page and again on the detailed opening page, supporting application initiation from multiple points.

requirements.text: The system shall support an application workflow that can be launched for a specific opening after the applicant has identified the relevant park and role.
The system shall communicate employment perks, benefits, or similar value propositions alongside job discovery content.
requirements.annotation_notes: Several screens include perks and benefits messaging or benefit bullets, but full truth of those claims depends on policies beyond screenshot evidence.

requirements.text: The system shall provide an alternative discovery path for corporate or non-park-specific careers.
requirements.annotation_notes: The careers pages include a distinct View Corporate Careers action, indicating that applicants are not limited to a single park-based path.

requirements.text: The system shall support narrowing opportunities within a selected park through structured categories or similar organization rather than only a flat undifferentiated job list.
The system shall surface recruiting campaigns or hiring events associated with a park's employment opportunities.
